title: Emoji sanitizer investigation
body: Initial debugging session for the unicode sanitizer test failure. Used a test string containing '👨‍👩‍👧‍👦' (family emoji with zero-width joiners). The sanitizer appears to strip ZWJ sequences incorrectly, treating them as separate characters instead of preserving the joined presentation. Noticed that U+200D (ZWJ) is sometimes dropped, causing the grapheme cluster to break. The assertion in test_unicode_sanitizer expects the full sequence to be preserved, but the output shows individual emoji characters without joiners. Need to investigate the sanitizer's handling of ZWJ and possibly related variation selectors. Logs show a regex pattern that may be removing joiners as unwanted control characters. Plan to review the sanitizer implementation in the unicode module.
